title: What Made Their Meals Extra Nourishing
subtitle: 全球健康饮食的秘密与一个共同因素

paragraphs.en: To write his 2010 book, The 5-Factor World Diet, nutritionist Harley Pasternak traveled to the healthiest countries around the world to learn more about what made their meals extra nourishing. He noted that Japanese people ate a wonderful variety of seaweeds, and that Chinese people tried to include at least five different colors in every meal.
paragraphs.zh: 为了撰写2010年出版的《五要素世界饮食》一书，营养学家哈利·帕斯特纳克走访了全球最健康的一些国家，探寻是什么让当地饮食格外滋养身心。他注意到日本人会吃多种多样的海藻，而中国人则力求每餐包含至少五种不同颜色的食材。

paragraphs.en: Pasternak also came away with some valuable observations about how different the North American way of life was, compared with many other countries. For starters, Americans eat much bigger portions than people in other countries. "We don't prioritize eating seasonally or locally, and we also add lots of salt, sugar and thickening agents to our foods," explained Pasternak.
paragraphs.zh: 帕斯特纳克还观察到北美生活方式与其他许多国家存在巨大差异。首先，美国人的进食分量远大于其他国家的人。帕斯特纳克解释道："我们不注重吃应季或本地的食物，还会在食品中添加大量盐、糖和增稠剂。"

words.meaning: v. 优先考虑；给……优先权

paragraphs.en: Contrast that to the healthy Mediterranean, Nordic and Okinawan diets listed in Pasternak's book. They all seem to stick to the ethos of regional, seasonal produce. For example, a traditional Mediterranean diet includes fruits, vegetables, whole grains, nuts and olive oil as the main components of nutritional intake.
paragraphs.zh: 这与帕斯特纳克书中列举的健康的地中海饮食、北欧饮食和冲绳饮食形成鲜明对比。这些饮食模式似乎都坚守着本地、应季食材的理念。例如，传统地中海饮食以水果、蔬菜、全谷物、坚果和橄榄油作为营养摄入的主要组成部分。

paragraphs.en: Fish, chicken and red wine make moderate appearances, while red meat, salt and sugar are used much less often. The benefits of a traditional Mediterranean diet have been studied since the 1970s, and researchers have found that living that olive oil life can help people lose weight, lower their heart disease risk and reverse diabetes. Most other healthy eating cultures also make meals an event — say, multiple courses around the family table, or a glass or two of red wine at a long lunch — as opposed to hastily wolfing down handfuls of cereal above the kitchen sink and calling it dinner. Each of the healthy eating cultures has its own unique feature.
paragraphs.zh: 鱼肉、鸡肉和红酒则适量出现，红肉、盐和糖的使用频率则低得多。自20世纪70年代以来，人们一直在研究传统地中海饮食的益处，研究人员发现，以橄榄油为主的生活方式有助于减重、降低心脏病风险和逆转糖尿病。其他大多数健康饮食文化也把吃饭当作一件正经事——比如全家围坐享用多道菜肴，或是午餐时慢慢喝上一两杯红酒——而不是在水槽边匆匆吞下几把麦片就权当晚餐。每一种健康饮食文化都有其独特之处。

paragraphs.en: But Pasternak did take note of one unifying factor in all of the healthy societies he observed. "The only overlapping feature in most of these healthy countries is that they all walk way more than the average American," said Pasternak. "So really, regardless of what you're eating, if someone's walking four miles more than you each day, they're going to be a lot thinner and live a lot longer than you."
paragraphs.zh: 但帕斯特纳克确实注意到了他所观察的健康社会中一个共通的因素。"这些健康国家唯一重叠的特征就是，他们的步行量都远超普通美国人，"帕斯特纳克说道。"所以说，无论你吃的是什么，如果有人每天比你多走四英里，他们就一定会比你瘦得多，也活得长得多。"
